title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs threat models, untrusted content, xss, paths, templates, and csp from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for explicit static-publishing trust and security policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Threat Model · changed case · Review Threat Model
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 3430 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.

Which action demonstrates Threat Model without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Threat Model · changed case · Review Threat Model.

stimulus.title: Defense In Depth case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 4144 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A restrictive CSP repairs unsafe generated HTML.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers. Artifact under review: explicit static-publishing trust and security policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Defense In Depth, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Defense In Depth: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 5763 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth, keep Defense In Depth connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Defense In Depth precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review publishing team handles case 5763 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.

For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Defense In Depth, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A restrictive CSP repairs unsafe generated HTML.
